title: Neural general circulation models for weather and climate
methods: differentiable dynamical core (pseudo-spectral hydrostatic primitive equations in JAX)
learned neural-network physics parameterization (single-column fully connected network)
end-to-end online training with multi-step rollout gradient backpropagation
stochastic ensemble via Gaussian random field noise injection
WeatherBench2 evaluation (RMSE, RMSB, CRPS, spread-skill ratio)
core_conclusion: NeuralGCM combines a differentiable atmospheric dynamical core with end-to-end learned neural-network parameterizations, achieving forecast accuracy on par with the best physics-based and pure machine-learning models across 1- to 15-day weather and multi-decade climate timescales. It produces calibrated ensemble forecasts with better CRPS than ECMWF-ENS, realistically simulates tropical cyclones and 40-year global temperature trends, and delivers 3–5 orders of magnitude computational savings over conventional GCMs.
why_it_matters: First ML model with calibrated ensemble forecasts (spread-skill ratio ~1, better CRPS than ECMWF-ENS); first hybrid model stable for 40-year AMIP simulations; empirically supports that weather-scale training is sufficient for climate parameterization; orders-of-magnitude computational savings enabling large-ensemble climate projections; fully open-source infrastructure.
why_nature: Paradigm-shifting result demonstrating end-to-end deep learning is compatible with tasks performed by decades-mature GCMs; surpasses ECMWF ensemble on ensemble probability scores; direct societal relevance to disaster preparedness and climate adaptation; platform contribution with open-source code and WeatherBench2 integration.
added_value_to_my_research: NeuralGCM downscaled to neighborhood scale via generative ML provides continuous climate exposure covariates for health geography and 2SFCA accessibility models
Graph-neural-network physics parameterization with conservation-law constraints as template for physics-informed GeoAI graph learning in urban and epidemic models
NeuralGCM-ENS probabilistic outputs enable climate-aware dynamic catchment delineation in R2SFCA models, propagating atmospheric forecast uncertainty to health-facility accessibility
Online end-to-end training paradigm for subgrid parameterization translates to learned SIR/compartment rates conditioned on local spatial context for disease-spread modeling
40-year NeuralGCM ensemble as cheap dynamic climate covariates for Monte Carlo scenarios of climate-driven population shift and infrastructure accessibility planning

related.title: Probabilistic weather forecasting with machine learning
related.relation: companion probabilistic ML weather forecasting approach (AIFS-CRPS), provides epistemic uncertainty framework complementary to NeuralGCM-ENS

related.title: Fast, scale-adaptive and uncertainty-aware downscaling of Earth system model fields with generative machine learning
related.relation: generative downscaling of coarse GCM/ESM outputs to high resolution — directly extends NeuralGCM's coarse-resolution climate fields

related.title: Multiscale simulations of complex systems by learning their effective dynamics
related.relation: general framework for learning effective coarse-grained dynamics of complex systems — NeuralGCM is its atmospheric instantiation

related.title: Learning the coupled dynamics of global climate modes
related.relation: ML modeling of coupled ocean-atmosphere climate mode dynamics that NeuralGCM could inform or be informed by

related.title: Automating turbulence modelling by multi-agent reinforcement learning
related.relation: ML-based replacement of turbulence parameterizations in fluid simulations — closely related challenge to NeuralGCM's learned physics module

figures.caption: Structure of the NeuralGCM model — overall architecture showing encoding, dynamical core, learned physics module, and ODE time-stepping
Weather forecasting accuracy scores (RMSE, RMSB, CRPS, spread-skill ratio) comparing NeuralGCM variants with ECMWF-HRES, ECMWF-ENS, GraphCast, and Pangu
Case study of Hurricane Laura (2020-08-22): 1-, 5-, 10-day forecasts of specific humidity at 700 hPa and ensemble member tropical cyclone tracks
Climate simulation results: global mean temperature seasonality, 40-year AMIP trends, RMSB vs CMIP6, tropical temperature profiles, tropical cyclone tracks, precipitable water bias
